name: Kawainui Marsh
tagline: Hawaii's largest freshwater marsh — flat, stroller-friendly, and a birder's paradise.
parking: Free lot at Kaha Park on Kailua Rd
bestTime: Early morning for best birdwatching; cooler temps
talkStory: At 830 acres, Kawainui is Hawaii's largest remaining freshwater marsh and was once a fishpond system that fed thousands of ancient Hawaiians — it was among the most productive aquaculture sites in all of Polynesia. In the 1960s developers nearly filled it in for a housing subdivision, but community activists fought for decades to protect it. The marsh is now a critical habitat for endangered Hawaiian waterbirds including the ae'o (stilt), 'alae 'ula (moorhen), and 'alae ke'oke'o (coot). The paved path is fully accessible — combine with a Kailua Beach day.
